title: goodenia watsonii subsp. glandulosa
intro: Goodenia watsonii subsp. glandulosa is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Goodenia watsonii plant, belonging to the Goodeniaceae family of flowering plants native to Australia. It has been traditionally used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as wounds and dermatitis. Goodenia watsonii subsp. glandulosa can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, although its use and preparation require caution and should be guided by experienced practitioners or medical professionals.
